headline_en: Talent Shift, GPU Reality Check, and Microsoft's Cross-Pacific AI Play
lede_en: Today's AI landscape is marked by a major talent move as AlphaFold lead John Jumper leaves DeepMind for Anthropic, a dispelling of the GPU commoditization myth, and Microsoft's role as a bidirectional reseller of AI models between the US and China. Simultaneously, advances in agent engineering tools and infrastructure debates underscore the rapid maturation of the ecosystem.

sections_en.title: Geopolitics & Policy
sections_en.body: Microsoft is bridging the US-China AI divide by reselling ChatGPT to Chinese enterprises while testing China's DeepSeek models for Western clients, creating a cross-Pacific trade network that circumvents tech restrictions. Meanwhile, a proposed US bill takes aim at government pressure on platforms to censor speech, and a strong warning against restricting open-source AI argues that such moves would undermine competition and safety—showing how commercial strategy, free speech, and model access are becoming tightly intertwined in global AI policy.

sections_en.title: Infrastructure & Scaling Realities
sections_en.body: The assumption that GPU compute would commoditize is debunked as the opposite occurs: gigawatt-scale AI factories face immense physical and financial challenges, ensuring persistent compute scarcity. This dovetails with the data blackhole—models remain sample-inefficient, forcing reliance on synthetic data and distillation to keep open-source within months of closed models. On the ground, comparisons between OpenRouter and LiteLLM highlight the cost, control, and privacy tradeoffs teams must navigate to orchestrate multi-provider access efficiently.

sections_en.title: Agent Engineering & Automation
sections_en.body: Agent design is maturing rapidly. Anthropic's skills-based Claude Code architecture enables composable capabilities, while NVIDIA's SpatialClaw gives vision-language models training-free spatial reasoning via code interfaces. Cloudflare's temporary Workers accounts let agents deploy instantly, and a flurry of tools—YouTube notetaker, Qwen-code fixes, slide-rendering fixes—reinforce reliability. Figure's humanoid robot count surpassing its human employees signals a tangible shift toward embodied, automated labor at scale, underscoring that agentic AI is moving from concept to operation.

headline_zh: 今日共采集 17 项内容
lede_zh: 今日共采集 17 项内容。
